title: senecio flammeus var. rufus
intro: Senecio flammeus var. rufus, also known as Red Groundsel, is a variety of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to South Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its sesquiterpene lactone content. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Red Groundsel can be dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be carefully considered due to potential toxicity.
